name: Linode / Akamai Cloud
what_it_does: Affordable cloud platform (now Akamai Cloud) offering VPS, managed Kubernetes, managed databases, and object storage with a well-documented REST API, backed by Akamai's global CDN network.
use_cases: Deploying cost-effective compute instances for agent workloads with global presence
Automating managed database provisioning (MySQL, PostgreSQL) via API
Building agent-driven infrastructure with object storage (S3-compatible) integration
Managing DNS and networking resources as part of infrastructure automation
not_for: Workloads needing GPU compute (limited GPU offerings vs AWS/GCP)
Deep integration with AWS/GCP managed services ecosystem
Organizations needing FedRAMP or healthcare compliance certifications
Complex multi-cloud networking with native hyperscaler connectivity
best_when: You want a developer-friendly, affordable cloud with managed Kubernetes and databases, and benefit from Akamai's CDN integration for edge delivery.
avoid_when: You need extensive managed services beyond compute and databases, strong EU data sovereignty, or advanced ML infrastructure.

auth.notes: Personal access tokens with configurable scopes (read, read_write per resource type). OAuth available for third-party apps. Scoped tokens are recommended for agent workflows.

pricing.paid_tiers: Nanode from $5/month; Standard from $10/month; LKE free control plane + node costs
pricing.notes: Competitive pricing with hourly billing. New accounts receive free credits. No ongoing free tier but pricing is among the most affordable for comparable specs.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns standardized error objects with errors array containing field and reason. Detailed and specific — good for agent parsing.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Linode creation with same label creates new instance (not idempotent). Most update operations are idempotent. Boot/reboot operations are effectively idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limit of 800 requests per 2 minutes is moderate — high-frequency automation needs rate limiting
Linode IDs are numeric integers, not human-readable — agents should map labels to IDs for usability
Instance provisioning takes 30-90 seconds — poll status until 'running' before attempting SSH or further config
Managed database provisioning takes several minutes — much longer than compute instance creation
Region IDs use slug format (us-east, eu-west) — verify slug availability as not all services exist in all regions
